fields.title: Reading Lesson
fields.text_content:
📖 INTRODUCTION
In our daily life, we use numbers everywhere — counting students in class, money in pocket, or population of a city. When numbers get very big, we need a proper system to read and write them.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔑 KEY CONCEPTS

1. Place Value System
Every digit in a number has a place value.
Example: In 45,678
→ 4 is in Ten-Thousands place = 40,000
→ 5 is in Thousands place = 5,000
→ 6 is in Hundreds place = 600
→ 7 is in Tens place = 70
→ 8 is in Ones place = 8

2. Indian Number System
We group numbers using commas:
→ First comma after 3 digits from right
→ Then every 2 digits
Example: 75,23,456 = 75 Lakhs, 23 Thousands, 456

3. Face Value vs Place Value
→ Face Value = the digit itself
→ Place Value = digit × its position value
Example: In 6,789 — Face value of 6 = 6, Place value of 6 = 6,000

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

➕ FORMULAS
→ 10 Ones = 1 Ten = 10
→ 10 Tens = 1 Hundred = 100
→ 10 Hundreds = 1 Thousand = 1,000
→ 100 Thousands = 1 Lakh = 1,00,000
→ 100 Lakhs = 1 Crore = 1,00,00,000
→ Difference = Place Value - Face Value

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

💡 REAL LIFE EXAMPLES

Example 1: Your school fee is Rs. 1,00,000 per year = ONE LAKH rupees!

Example 2: A cricket stadium holds 1,00,000 fans = ONE LAKH fans!

Example 3: A big city has 1,00,00,000 people = 1 CRORE people!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 SOLVED EXAMPLES

Q1. Write 75,00,000 in words.
Step 1: 75 is in lakhs place
Answer: Seventy Five Lakhs ✅

Q2. Place value of 4 in 3,45,678?
Step 1: 4 is in Ten-Thousands place
Answer: 4 × 10,000 = 40,000 ✅

Q3. Which is greater — 4,56,789 or 4,65,789?
Step 1: Compare digit by digit
Step 2: 6 > 5 at ten-thousands place
Answer: 4,65,789 is greater ✅

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

⭐ REMEMBER THIS!
→ Largest 4-digit number = 9,999
→ Smallest 5-digit number = 10,000
→ Largest 5-digit number = 99,999
→ Smallest 6-digit number = 1,00,000
→ 1 Lakh = 1,00,000 (5 zeros)
→ 1 Crore = 1,00,00,000 (7 zeros)

Rounding Rule:
→ If digit < 5 → round DOWN
→ If digit ≥ 5 → round UP

fields.title: Reading Lesson
fields.text_content:
📖 INTRODUCTION
Whole numbers are the numbers we use for counting — 0, 1, 2, 3, 4... They include zero and all positive numbers but NO fractions or decimals.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔑 KEY CONCEPTS

1. What are Whole Numbers?
→ Natural Numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5...
→ Whole Numbers: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5...
→ Only difference: Whole numbers include ZERO!

2. Number Line
→ Draw a line, mark 0 in middle
→ Right side = positive numbers (1, 2, 3...)
→ Every number has a successor (next number)
→ Every number except 0 has a predecessor (previous number)

3. Properties of Whole Numbers
→ Closure: a + b is always a whole number
→ Commutative: a + b = b + a
→ Associative: (a+b)+c = a+(b+c)
→ Distributive: a×(b+c) = a×b + a×c

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

➕ FORMULAS
→ Successor of n = n + 1
→ Predecessor of n = n - 1
→ Smallest whole number = 0
→ There is NO largest whole number
→ a × 0 = 0 (any number × 0 = 0)
→ a × 1 = a (any number × 1 = itself)
→ a + 0 = a (any number + 0 = itself)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

💡 REAL LIFE EXAMPLES

Example 1: You have 5 apples, eat 5 = 0 apples left. 0 is a whole number!

Example 2: Class has 30 students. 30 is a whole number.

Example 3: A shop has 0 items left = 0 is a whole number!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 SOLVED EXAMPLES

Q1. What is successor of 99?
Answer: 99 + 1 = 100 ✅

Q2. What is predecessor of 1000?
Answer: 1000 - 1 = 999 ✅

Q3. Is 0 a natural number?
Answer: NO! 0 is a whole number but NOT a natural number ✅

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

⭐ REMEMBER THIS!
→ Whole Numbers = 0, 1, 2, 3, 4...
→ Natural Numbers = 1, 2, 3, 4... (no zero!)
→ Smallest Whole Number = 0
→ Smallest Natural Number = 1
→ 0 is even number
→ Every natural number is a whole number
→ Every whole number is NOT a natural number (0 is exception)

fields.title: Reading Lesson
fields.text_content:
📖 INTRODUCTION
Numbers have special properties — some can only be divided by 1 and themselves, while others have many factors. This chapter helps us understand these properties!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔑 KEY CONCEPTS

1. Factors
→ A factor divides a number exactly (no remainder)
→ Example: Factors of 12 = 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12
→ 1 is a factor of every number
→ Every number is a factor of itself

2. Multiples
→ Multiples = multiplication table of a number
→ Example: Multiples of 3 = 3, 6, 9, 12, 15...
→ Every number is a multiple of itself
→ Multiples are infinite (never ending)

3. Prime Numbers
→ Has exactly 2 factors: 1 and itself
→ Examples: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, 19...
→ 2 is the ONLY even prime number
→ 1 is NOT a prime number

4. Composite Numbers
→ Has more than 2 factors
→ Examples: 4, 6, 8, 9, 10, 12...
→ 4 = factors are 1, 2, 4 (3 factors)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

➕ FORMULAS
→ HCF = Highest Common Factor
→ LCM = Lowest Common Multiple
→ HCF × LCM = Product of two numbers
→ Prime factorization: write number as product of primes
→ Divisibility by 2: last digit is 0,2,4,6,8
→ Divisibility by 3: sum of digits divisible by 3
→ Divisibility by 5: last digit is 0 or 5
→ Divisibility by 10: last digit is 0

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

💡 REAL LIFE EXAMPLES

Example 1: 24 students divided into equal groups — factors of 24 tell us possible group sizes!

Example 2: Buses come every 6 mins, trains every 9 mins — LCM tells when both come together!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📝 SOLVED EXAMPLES

Q1. Find factors of 18.
Factors: 1, 2, 3, 6, 9, 18 ✅

Q2. Is 37 prime?
Factors of 37: only 1 and 37
Answer: YES, 37 is prime ✅

Q3. Find HCF of 12 and 18.
12 = 2×2×3, 18 = 2×3×3
HCF = 2×3 = 6 ✅

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

⭐ REMEMBER THIS!
→ 1 is neither prime nor composite
→ 2 is the smallest and only even prime
→ All even numbers > 2 are composite
→ Prime numbers up to 20: 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19
→ HCF is always ≤ smaller number
→ LCM is always ≥ larger number

fields.question_text: How many zeros are in 1 lakh?
fields.explanation: 1 lakh is written as 1,00,000 in the Indian number system. Count the zeros after 1 — you will find exactly 5 zeros. So the answer is B (5 zeros).

fields.question_text: Which is the smallest 4-digit number?
fields.explanation: 4-digit numbers start from 1000 and go up to 9999. The very first 4-digit number is 1000. Numbers below 1000 like 999 are 3-digit numbers.

fields.question_text: What is the place value of 5 in 35,947?
fields.explanation: In 35,947 — starting from right: 7 is ones, 4 is tens, 9 is hundreds, 5 is thousands. So place value of 5 = 5 × 1000 = 5000.

fields.question_text: How many thousands make one lakh?
fields.explanation: 1 lakh = 1,00,000. 1 thousand = 1,000. So 1,00,000 ÷ 1,000 = 100. Therefore 100 thousands make one lakh.

fields.question_text: Which number is greatest — 4567, 4576, 4675, 4657?
fields.explanation: All numbers start with 4. Compare next digit — 4567(5), 4576(5), 4675(6), 4657(6). Between 4675 and 4657, compare third digit — 7 > 5. So 4675 is greatest.

fields.question_text: Round off 4836 to nearest hundred
fields.explanation: To round to nearest hundred, look at tens digit. In 4836, tens digit is 3. Since 3 is less than 5, we round DOWN. So 4836 rounds down to 4800.

fields.question_text: 1 crore = how many lakhs?
fields.explanation: 1 crore = 1,00,00,000 and 1 lakh = 1,00,000. So 1 crore ÷ 1 lakh = 1,00,00,000 ÷ 1,00,000 = 100. Therefore 1 crore = 100 lakhs.

fields.question_text: What is the successor of 99,999?
fields.explanation: Successor means the next number. To find successor, add 1 to the given number. 99,999 + 1 = 1,00,000. So the successor of 99,999 is 1,00,000.

fields.question_text: Which symbol separates periods in Indian number system?
fields.explanation: In the Indian number system, we use commas to separate number periods. Example: 1,00,000 — the commas separate ones period, thousands period and lakhs period.

fields.question_text: Estimate 578 + 321 to nearest hundred
fields.explanation: Round each number to nearest hundred first. 578 rounds to 600 (since 78 > 50). 321 rounds to 300 (since 21 < 50). Then add: 600 + 300 = 900.

fields.question_text: What is the difference between place value and face value of 6 in 56,789?
fields.explanation: Face value of 6 = 6 (always the digit itself). Place value of 6 in 56,789 = 6 is in thousands place = 6,000. Difference = Place value - Face value = 6,000 - 6 = 5,994.

fields.question_text: In International system, 75,00,000 is written as?
fields.explanation: 75,00,000 in Indian system = 75 lakhs. In International system, groups of 3 digits from right. 7,500,000 = 7 million 500 thousand. This is correct international format.

fields.question_text: Largest 5-digit number using digits 3,1,0,7,5 without repetition?
fields.explanation: To make largest number, arrange digits in descending order. Digits: 7,5,3,1,0. Arranged from largest to smallest = 7,5,3,1,0 = 75310. This is the largest possible 5-digit number.

fields.question_text: Estimate 8,74,573 to nearest lakh
fields.explanation: To round to nearest lakh, look at ten-thousands digit. In 8,74,573 the ten-thousands digit is 7. Since 7 ≥ 5, we round UP. So 8,74,573 rounds up to 9,00,000.

fields.question_text: How many 6-digit numbers are there in total?
fields.explanation: 6-digit numbers range from 1,00,000 to 9,99,999. Total count = 9,99,999 - 1,00,000 + 1 = 9,00,000. So there are exactly 9 lakh 6-digit numbers.

fields.question_text: What is 10 million in Indian system?
fields.explanation: 1 million = 10 lakhs. So 10 million = 10 × 10 lakhs = 100 lakhs = 1 crore = 1,00,00,000. Therefore 10 million = 1 crore in Indian system.

fields.question_text: Predecessor of 10,00,000 is?
fields.explanation: Predecessor means the number that comes just before. To find predecessor, subtract 1. 10,00,000 - 1 = 9,99,999. So predecessor of 10,00,000 is 9,99,999.

fields.question_text: Round off 56,78,459 to nearest ten lakh
fields.explanation: To round to nearest ten lakh, look at lakhs digit. In 56,78,459 the lakhs digit is 6. Since 6 ≥ 5, round UP the ten lakhs digit from 5 to 6. So answer is 60,00,000.

fields.question_text: Which number has digit 4 with place value 40,000?
fields.explanation: Place value 40,000 means 4 is in ten-thousands place. In 3,45,678 — from right: 8(ones), 7(tens), 6(hundreds), 5(thousands), 4(ten-thousands). So 4 has place value 40,000. Answer is A.

fields.question_text: Smallest 6-digit number using 1,2,0,3,4,5 without repetition?
fields.explanation: Smallest number: arrange digits ascending but 0 cannot be first digit. Digits: 0,1,2,3,4,5. Start with smallest non-zero = 1, then arrange rest ascending: 1,0,2,3,4,5 = 1,02,345.

fields.question_text: What is the smallest whole number?
fields.explanation: Whole numbers start from 0 and go up — 0, 1, 2, 3, 4... So the smallest whole number is 0. Note that natural numbers start from 1, but whole numbers include 0.

fields.question_text: What is the successor of 999?
fields.explanation: Successor means the next number. Add 1 to find successor. 999 + 1 = 1000. So the successor of 999 is 1000.

fields.question_text: What is the predecessor of 100?
fields.explanation: Predecessor means the number that comes just before. Subtract 1 to find predecessor. 100 - 1 = 99. So the predecessor of 100 is 99.

fields.question_text: Which of these is NOT a whole number?
fields.explanation: Whole numbers are 0, 1, 2, 3, 4... They are always zero or positive. Negative numbers like -3 are NOT whole numbers. They belong to integers.

fields.question_text: What is 5 + 0 = ?
fields.explanation: Zero is the additive identity — adding 0 to any number gives the same number. So 5 + 0 = 5. This is called the identity property of addition.

fields.question_text: What is 7 × 0 = ?
fields.explanation: Any number multiplied by 0 is always 0. This is the zero property of multiplication. So 7 × 0 = 0.

fields.question_text: Which property does 3 + 4 = 4 + 3 show?
fields.explanation: Commutative property means order of numbers can be swapped without changing the result. 3 + 4 = 4 + 3 = 7. This works for both addition and multiplication.

fields.question_text: Is 0 a natural number?
fields.explanation: No! 0 is NOT a natural number. Natural numbers start from 1 — 1, 2, 3, 4... Whole numbers include 0 but natural numbers do not. This is the key difference.

fields.question_text: What is 8 × 1 = ?
fields.explanation: 1 is the multiplicative identity — multiplying any number by 1 gives the same number. So 8 × 1 = 8. This is called the identity property of multiplication.

fields.question_text: On number line, which side has larger numbers?
fields.explanation: On a number line, numbers increase as we move to the right. So right side always has larger numbers. Numbers decrease as we move left. 0 is usually at the center.

fields.question_text: Which property: (2+3)+4 = 2+(3+4)?
fields.explanation: Associative property means grouping of numbers can change without affecting the result. (2+3)+4 = 5+4 = 9 and 2+(3+4) = 2+7 = 9. Both equal 9 — this is associative property.

fields.question_text: Which largest number has no successor?
fields.explanation: Every whole number has a successor — just add 1. There is no largest whole number because we can always add 1 to get a bigger number. So no such number exists.

fields.question_text: Using distributive property: 4 × (5 + 3) = ?
fields.explanation: Distributive property: a×(b+c) = a×b + a×c. So 4×(5+3) = 4×5 + 4×3 = 20 + 12 = 32. You can also solve directly: 4×8 = 32. Both give same answer.

fields.question_text: How many whole numbers are between 10 and 20?
fields.explanation: Between 10 and 20 means not including 10 and 20. Numbers are: 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19. Count them — that is 9 whole numbers between 10 and 20.

fields.question_text: Which whole number has no predecessor?
fields.explanation: Predecessor means previous number. For 0, predecessor would be -1. But -1 is not a whole number! So 0 is the only whole number that has no predecessor in the set of whole numbers.

fields.question_text: Every natural number is a whole number — True or False?
fields.explanation: True! Natural numbers are 1,2,3,4... and whole numbers are 0,1,2,3,4... Every natural number is included in whole numbers. But reverse is not true — 0 is whole but not natural.

fields.question_text: Simplify using properties: 125 × 40 + 125 × 60
fields.explanation: Using distributive property: 125×40 + 125×60 = 125×(40+60) = 125×100 = 12,500. This is much faster than calculating each separately!

fields.question_text: What is the product of the predecessor and successor of 10?
fields.explanation: Predecessor of 10 = 10-1 = 9. Successor of 10 = 10+1 = 11. Product = 9 × 11 = 99. Notice: (n-1)(n+1) = n²-1 = 100-1 = 99. This is a useful pattern!

fields.question_text: Which property: a × (b × c) = (a × b) × c?
fields.explanation: Associative property of multiplication: grouping does not change the product. a×(b×c) = (a×b)×c. Example: 2×(3×4) = 2×12 = 24 and (2×3)×4 = 6×4 = 24. Same answer!

fields.question_text: Find the value: 99 × 101 using properties
fields.explanation: 99 × 101 = 99 × (100+1) = 99×100 + 99×1 = 9900 + 99 = 9999. Using distributive property makes this calculation easy without long multiplication!

fields.question_text: Which of these is a prime number?
fields.explanation: A prime number has exactly 2 factors: 1 and itself. 11 has only factors 1 and 11 — so it is prime. 9=3×3, 15=3×5, 21=3×7 all have more than 2 factors.

fields.question_text: How many factors does 12 have?
fields.explanation: Factors of 12 are: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 12. Count them — that is 6 factors. Remember: 1×12=12, 2×6=12, 3×4=12. Each pair gives 2 factors.

fields.question_text: What is the HCF of 12 and 18?
fields.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Common factors: 1,2,3,6. Highest Common Factor = 6. So HCF(12,18) = 6.

fields.question_text: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
fields.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4,8,12,16,20,24... Multiples of 6: 6,12,18,24... First common multiple = 12. So LCM(4,6) = 12.

fields.question_text: Is 1 a prime number?
fields.explanation: No! 1 is NOT a prime number. Prime numbers have exactly 2 factors. But 1 has only 1 factor (itself). So 1 is neither prime nor composite — it is a special number.

fields.question_text: Which is divisible by 3?
fields.explanation: Divisibility rule for 3: sum of digits must be divisible by 3. 135: 1+3+5=9, and 9÷3=3. So 135 is divisible by 3. Check others: 124→7, 112→4, 143→8 — none divisible by 3.

fields.question_text: Which is the only even prime number?
fields.explanation: 2 is the only even prime number! All other even numbers are divisible by 2 (besides 1 and themselves), so they have more than 2 factors and are composite. 2 has only factors 1 and 2.

fields.question_text: What are multiples of 5 between 1 and 30?
fields.explanation: Multiples of 5: 5,10,15,20,25,30,35... Between 1 and 30 INCLUDING both ends if they qualify: 5,10,15,20,25,30. The question says between 1 and 30 which includes 30.

fields.question_text: Which number is divisible by both 2 and 5?
fields.explanation: Divisible by 2: must end in 0,2,4,6,8. Divisible by 5: must end in 0 or 5. For both conditions: must end in 0. Among options, 30 ends in 0. So 30 is divisible by both 2 and 5.

fields.question_text: What is the prime factorization of 24?
fields.explanation: Prime factorization means breaking into prime number factors only. 24 = 2×12 = 2×2×6 = 2×2×2×3. All factors (2 and 3) are prime numbers. So 24 = 2³×3.

fields.question_text: HCF of two numbers is 6 and LCM is 36. One number is 12. Find other.
fields.explanation: Formula: HCF × LCM = Product of two numbers. 6 × 36 = 12 × other number. 216 = 12 × other. Other number = 216 ÷ 12 = 18. Verify: HCF(12,18)=6 ✓ LCM(12,18)=36 ✓

fields.question_text: How many prime numbers are between 1 and 20?
fields.explanation: Prime numbers between 1 and 20: 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. Count them: that is 8 prime numbers. Remember: 1 is not prime, 4,6,8,9,10,12,14,15,16,18,20 are composite.

fields.question_text: Which is divisible by 11?
fields.explanation: Divisibility by 11: difference of alternating digit sums must be 0 or 11. 121: (1+1)-2=0 ✓. 132: (1+2)-3=0 ✓. 143: (1+3)-4=0 ✓. All three are divisible by 11!

fields.question_text: Find LCM of 12, 15 and 20.
A number is divisible by 9. Is it always divisible by 3?
fields.explanation: Yes! If a number is divisible by 9, it is always divisible by 3. Because 9 = 3×3, so any multiple of 9 is also a multiple of 3. Example: 27÷9=3 and 27÷3=9. Both work!

fields.question_text: Express 72 as product of prime factors.
fields.explanation: 72 = 2×36 = 2×2×18 = 2×2×2×9 = 2×2×2×3×3 = 2³×3². Only option C shows complete prime factorization with all prime factors. Other options contain composite numbers.

fields.question_text: Bells ring every 3, 4 and 5 minutes. When will all ring together again?
fields.explanation: Find LCM(3,4,5). 3=3, 4=2², 5=5. LCM = 2²×3×5 = 60. All bells will ring together after 60 minutes. LCM gives us the time when all events coincide.

fields.question_text: What is HCF of 0 and 5?
fields.explanation: HCF of 0 and any number n is n itself. Because every number divides 0 (0÷5=0 remainder 0). So the highest factor common to 0 and 5 is 5 itself. HCF(0,5) = 5.

fields.question_text: Which smallest number is divisible by 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6?
fields.explanation: Find LCM(2,3,4,5,6). 2=2, 3=3, 4=2², 5=5, 6=2×3. LCM = 2²×3×5 = 60. So 60 is the smallest number divisible by all of 2,3,4,5 and 6.

fields.question_text: Co-prime numbers have HCF equal to?
fields.explanation: Co-prime numbers (also called relatively prime) are pairs of numbers whose HCF is 1. Example: 8 and 9 are co-prime because HCF(8,9)=1. They need not be prime individually.

fields.question_text: How many points are needed to draw a line?
fields.explanation: A line passes through at least 2 points. With just 1 point, we can only draw a dot. With 2 points, we can draw exactly one straight line passing through both points.

fields.question_text: A line segment has:
fields.explanation: A line segment is a part of a line with two fixed endpoints. Unlike a line which extends infinitely in both directions, a line segment has a definite start and end point.

fields.question_text: A ray has:
fields.explanation: A ray starts at one fixed point (called origin) and extends infinitely in one direction. It has exactly one endpoint — the starting point. Example: a ray of sunlight.

fields.question_text: Two lines that never meet are called:
fields.explanation: Parallel lines are lines in the same plane that never meet, no matter how far they are extended. The distance between parallel lines always remains the same. Example: railway tracks.

fields.question_text: An angle is formed by:
fields.option_b: Two rays with a common endpoint
fields.explanation: An angle is formed when two rays start from the same point (called the vertex). The two rays are called the arms of the angle. The common starting point is called the vertex.

fields.question_text: A polygon with 3 sides is called:
fields.explanation: A polygon is a closed figure made of line segments. Triangle has 3 sides and 3 angles. Square has 4 sides, Pentagon has 5 sides, Hexagon has 6 sides.

fields.question_text: The center of a circle is:
fields.option_c: A point equidistant from all points on circle
fields.explanation: The center of a circle is a fixed point inside the circle that is at equal distance (radius) from every point on the circle. All radii of a circle are equal in length.

fields.question_text: Diameter of a circle is:
fields.explanation: Diameter is the longest chord that passes through the center of the circle. Diameter = 2 × Radius. If radius = 5 cm, then diameter = 10 cm. Diameter divides circle into two equal halves.

fields.question_text: A quadrilateral has how many sides?
fields.explanation: Quadrilateral means four sides (quad = 4, lateral = sides). Examples of quadrilaterals: square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezium. All have exactly 4 sides and 4 angles.

fields.question_text: Lines meeting at a point are called:
fields.explanation: When two or more lines meet at a common point, they are called intersecting lines. The common point where they meet is called the point of intersection.

fields.question_text: A chord that passes through the center of a circle is called:
fields.explanation: A chord is a line segment joining two points on a circle. When a chord passes through the center, it becomes the diameter — the longest possible chord. Diameter = 2 × Radius.

fields.question_text: How many diagonals does a quadrilateral have?
fields.explanation: A diagonal joins two non-adjacent vertices of a polygon. In a quadrilateral ABCD, diagonals are AC and BD. So a quadrilateral has exactly 2 diagonals. A triangle has 0 diagonals.

fields.question_text: Which of these is an open curve?
fields.explanation: An open curve does not end where it starts — it has two different endpoints. A letter C shape is open. Circle, triangle and square are closed curves because they start and end at same point.

fields.question_text: The region inside a circle is called:
fields.explanation: The interior region of a circle including its boundary is called a disc. Arc is a part of the circle boundary. Sector is a pizza-slice shaped region. Chord is a line segment inside the circle.

fields.question_text: How many lines can pass through a single point?
fields.explanation: Through a single point, infinite number of lines can pass — in all possible directions. But through TWO points, exactly ONE line can be drawn. This is a fundamental concept in geometry.

fields.question_text: A hexagon has how many sides and diagonals?
fields.explanation: Hexagon has 6 sides. Formula for diagonals = n(n-3)/2 where n=sides. For hexagon: 6(6-3)/2 = 6×3/2 = 9 diagonals. So a hexagon has 6 sides and 9 diagonals.

fields.question_text: Two lines perpendicular to same line are:
fields.explanation: If two lines are both perpendicular to the same line, they are parallel to each other. They both make 90° with the original line and will never meet each other. Like two walls both perpendicular to the floor.

fields.question_text: The part of a circle between two radii is called:
fields.explanation: A sector is the region between two radii and the arc connecting them — like a pizza slice. A segment is region between a chord and arc. Arc is just the curved boundary part of circle.

fields.question_text: Which polygon has equal sides and equal angles?
fields.explanation: A regular polygon has all sides equal AND all angles equal. Examples: equilateral triangle (regular 3-gon), square (regular 4-gon), regular hexagon. Rectangle has equal angles but NOT equal sides.

fields.question_text: Concentric circles have:
fields.explanation: Concentric circles are circles that share the same center point but have different radii. Like rings on a target board — all circles have the same center but each ring has a different radius.

fields.question_text: How many degrees is a right angle?
fields.explanation: A right angle is exactly 90°. It looks like the corner of a square or the letter L. When two lines meet at 90°, they are called perpendicular lines. A right angle is marked with a small square symbol.

fields.question_text: An angle less than 90° is called:
fields.explanation: Acute angle is any angle that measures more than 0° but less than 90°. Examples: 30°, 45°, 60°, 89°. The word acute means sharp — acute angles look sharp and pointed.

fields.question_text: An angle greater than 90° but less than 180° is called:
fields.explanation: Obtuse angle measures more than 90° but less than 180°. Examples: 91°, 120°, 150°, 179°. The word obtuse means blunt — obtuse angles look wider and more open than right angles.

fields.question_text: A straight angle measures:
fields.explanation: A straight angle measures exactly 180°. It looks like a straight line. Two right angles together form a straight angle (90° + 90° = 180°). The arms of a straight angle point in opposite directions.

fields.question_text: A triangle with all sides equal is called:
fields.explanation: Equilateral triangle has all 3 sides equal and all 3 angles equal (each = 60°). Isosceles has 2 equal sides. Scalene has no equal sides. Right triangle has one 90° angle.

fields.question_text: How many sides does a pentagon have?
fields.explanation: Pentagon has 5 sides (penta = 5). Other polygons: triangle=3, quadrilateral=4, pentagon=5, hexagon=6, heptagon=7, octagon=8. The shape of a football field is sometimes pentagon shaped.

fields.question_text: A complete revolution is equal to:
fields.explanation: One complete revolution (full turn) = 360°. Half revolution = 180° (straight angle). Quarter revolution = 90° (right angle). Clock hands complete one revolution every 12 hours.

fields.question_text: Which instrument is used to measure angles?
fields.explanation: Protractor is used to measure and draw angles. It is a semicircular instrument marked from 0° to 180°. Ruler measures length. Compass draws circles. Set square helps draw right angles.

fields.question_text: A triangle with 2 equal sides is called:
fields.explanation: Isosceles triangle has exactly 2 equal sides and 2 equal angles (base angles). The word isosceles comes from Greek meaning equal legs. Equilateral has all 3 equal, scalene has none equal.

fields.question_text: A reflex angle measures:
fields.explanation: Reflex angle is greater than 180° but less than 360°. Examples: 200°, 270°, 350°. It is the larger angle when you go the long way around. Like the angle on the outside of an obtuse angle.

fields.question_text: Sum of all angles of a triangle is:
fields.explanation: The sum of all three angles of any triangle is always 180°. This is true for all triangles — equilateral, isosceles, scalene, right angled. Example: 60°+70°+50° = 180°.

fields.question_text: A quadrilateral has angle sum of:
fields.explanation: Sum of all angles of a quadrilateral = 360°. A quadrilateral can be divided into 2 triangles, each with 180°. So total = 2 × 180° = 360°. This works for all quadrilaterals.

fields.question_text: If two angles of a triangle are 60° and 70°, what is third angle?
fields.explanation: Sum of angles of triangle = 180°. Third angle = 180° - 60° - 70° = 180° - 130° = 50°. Always use the formula: Third angle = 180° - sum of other two angles.

fields.question_text: A quadrilateral with all sides equal and all angles 90° is:
fields.explanation: Square has all 4 sides equal AND all 4 angles = 90°. Rectangle has all angles 90° but sides not all equal. Rhombus has all sides equal but angles not 90°. Parallelogram has opposite sides equal.

fields.question_text: How many right angles make one complete revolution?
fields.explanation: One complete revolution = 360°. One right angle = 90°. Number of right angles = 360° ÷ 90° = 4. So 4 right angles make one complete revolution (full turn).

fields.question_text: A triangle cannot have more than one:
fields.explanation: A triangle cannot have more than one obtuse angle. If one angle is obtuse (>90°), the remaining two angles must add up to less than 90°, so both must be acute. Two obtuse angles would exceed 180°.

fields.question_text: In a right triangle, the two acute angles together measure:
fields.explanation: In a right triangle, one angle = 90°. Sum of all angles = 180°. So remaining two angles = 180° - 90° = 90°. The two acute angles always add up to exactly 90° in a right triangle.

fields.question_text: A parallelogram has opposite sides that are:
fields.explanation: In a parallelogram, opposite sides are equal in length AND parallel to each other. Opposite angles are also equal. Rectangle, square and rhombus are all special types of parallelograms.

fields.question_text: Which of these is NOT a quadrilateral?
fields.explanation: Pentagon has 5 sides so it is NOT a quadrilateral. Quadrilaterals have exactly 4 sides. Trapezium, Rhombus and Parallelogram all have 4 sides — they are all quadrilaterals.

fields.question_text: The angle made by clock hands at 3 o'clock is:
fields.explanation: At 3 o'clock, hour hand points to 3 (right side) and minute hand points to 12 (top). The angle between them is exactly 90° — a right angle. This is a classic example of a right angle in real life.

fields.question_text: Which of these is a negative integer?
fields.explanation: Negative integers are numbers less than zero written with a minus sign. -7 is a negative integer. 0 is neither positive nor negative. 5 and 3 are positive integers.

fields.question_text: On a number line, negative numbers are on which side?
fields.explanation: On a horizontal number line, negative numbers are always on the LEFT side of zero. Positive numbers are on the RIGHT side of zero. Zero is in the middle. As we move left, numbers decrease.

fields.question_text: What is the absolute value of -8?
fields.explanation: Absolute value means the distance from zero, always positive. Absolute value of -8 = 8. Absolute value of 8 = 8. Written as |-8| = 8. It is simply the number without its negative sign.

fields.question_text: Which is greater: -3 or -7?
fields.explanation: -3 is greater than -7. On number line, -3 is to the RIGHT of -7, so -3 > -7. Remember: with negative numbers, the one closer to zero is greater. -3 is closer to 0 than -7.
When you add a number and its negative, the result is always 0. (-5) + (+5) = 0. These are called additive inverses or opposites. They cancel each other out completely.

fields.question_text: Temperature fell from 4°C to -3°C. How many degrees did it fall?
fields.explanation: Change in temperature = Final - Initial = -3 - 4 = -7. The temperature fell by 7°C. We can also think: from 4°C to 0°C is 4 degrees, then from 0°C to -3°C is 3 more degrees. Total = 4+3 = 7°C.

fields.question_text: What is the additive inverse of -9?
fields.explanation: Additive inverse of a number is what you add to get zero. Additive inverse of -9 is +9 because (-9) + (+9) = 0. Additive inverse simply means changing the sign of the number.

fields.question_text: Which integer is neither positive nor negative?
fields.explanation: Zero (0) is neither positive nor negative. It is the only integer with this property. Zero is the boundary between positive and negative numbers on the number line.
When adding two negative integers, add their absolute values and keep the negative sign. |(-3)| + |(-4)| = 3+4 = 7. Keep negative sign → -7. So (-3) + (-4) = -7.

fields.question_text: Which is the smallest positive integer?
fields.explanation: Positive integers are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5... The smallest positive integer is 1. Zero is not a positive integer. There is no largest positive integer as they go on forever.

fields.question_text: What is 5 - (-3)?
fields.explanation: Subtracting a negative number is same as adding its positive. 5 - (-3) = 5 + 3 = 8. The rule is: minus × minus = plus. So subtracting -3 means adding +3.

fields.question_text: Arrange in ascending order: -5, 3, -1, 0, 2
fields.explanation: Ascending order means smallest to largest. On number line from left to right: -5 is leftmost (smallest), then -1, then 0, then 2, then 3 (largest). So: -5, -1, 0, 2, 3.

fields.question_text: A submarine is at -200m and rises 80m. New position?
fields.explanation: Starting position: -200m (below sea level). Rising 80m means adding 80. New position = -200 + 80 = -120m. The submarine is now at 120m below sea level. It is still below sea level.
(-8) - (-3) = (-8) + (+3) = -5. Rule: subtracting negative = adding positive. |-8| - |3| = 8-3 = 5. Since 8 > 3 and 8 is negative, answer is -5.

fields.question_text: Which property: (-5) + 0 = -5?
fields.explanation: Adding 0 to any integer gives the same integer. This is the Additive Identity property. 0 is the additive identity for integers. (-5) + 0 = -5, just as 5 + 0 = 5.

fields.question_text: The sum of an integer and its additive inverse is always:
fields.explanation: Additive inverse of any integer n is -n. Their sum = n + (-n) = 0. Always! Example: 7 + (-7) = 0, (-15) + 15 = 0. The sum is always zero regardless of which integer you choose.

fields.question_text: If today is Monday, what day was it 5 days ago?
fields.explanation: Monday - 5 days = Wednesday. Count backwards: Monday→Sunday(1)→Saturday(2)→Friday(3)→Thursday(4)→Wednesday(5). So 5 days before Monday is Wednesday. Integers help with such counting!

fields.question_text: Which is correct?
fields.explanation: 0 > -4 is correct. Zero is greater than all negative numbers. On number line, 0 is to the right of -4. Remember: on number line, right means greater. So 0 > -4 > -9.
Add all positives: +7. Add all negatives: (-4) + (-3) = -7. Then: (+7) + (-7) = 0. Or step by step: (-4)+(+7) = +3, then (+3)+(-3) = 0. Answer is 0.

fields.question_text: How many integers are between -4 and +4?
fields.explanation: Between -4 and +4 (not including -4 and +4): -3, -2, -1, 0, +1, +2, +3. Count them = 7 integers. The word between means we exclude the endpoints -4 and +4 themselves.

fields.question_text: In fraction 3/5, what is the numerator?
fields.explanation: In a fraction, numerator is the top number and denominator is the bottom number. In 3/5, numerator = 3 (top) and denominator = 5 (bottom). Numerator tells how many parts we have.

fields.question_text: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
fields.explanation: Equivalent fractions have same value. 4/8 = 4÷4 / 8÷4 = 1/2. So 4/8 is equivalent to 1/2. To find equivalent fractions, multiply or divide both numerator and denominator by same number.

fields.question_text: Which is a proper fraction?
fields.explanation: Proper fraction has numerator LESS THAN denominator. 3/7 → 3 < 7 ✓ proper fraction. Improper fractions: 7/3 and 9/4 (numerator > denominator). 5/5 = 1 is a whole number.

fields.question_text: What is 1/4 + 2/4?
fields.explanation: When denominators are same, just add numerators. 1/4 + 2/4 = (1+2)/4 = 3/4. The denominator stays the same. Never add denominators! Only add numerators when denominators match.

fields.question_text: Which fraction is greater: 3/4 or 2/3?
fields.explanation: Convert to same denominator. LCM(4,3)=12. 3/4 = 9/12 and 2/3 = 8/12. Since 9 > 8, we have 9/12 > 8/12, so 3/4 > 2/3. Always compare fractions by converting to same denominator.

fields.question_text: What is the simplest form of 6/8?
fields.explanation: To simplify, divide by HCF. HCF(6,8) = 2. So 6/8 = (6÷2)/(8÷2) = 3/4. A fraction is in simplest form when HCF of numerator and denominator is 1. HCF(3,4)=1, so 3/4 is simplest.

fields.question_text: Convert 7/3 to mixed fraction.
fields.explanation: Divide 7 by 3. 7 ÷ 3 = 2 remainder 1. Mixed fraction = quotient + remainder/divisor = 2 + 1/3 = 2 1/3. Always: improper fraction = whole part + fractional part.

fields.question_text: What fraction of an hour is 30 minutes?
fields.explanation: 1 hour = 60 minutes. 30 minutes as fraction of 1 hour = 30/60 = 1/2. So 30 minutes is half an hour = 1/2. This is a real life application of fractions.

fields.question_text: What is 3/5 of 25?
fields.explanation: 3/5 of 25 means (3/5) × 25 = (3 × 25)/5 = 75/5 = 15. First multiply numerator with the number, then divide by denominator. Or: 25/5 = 5, then 5 × 3 = 15.

fields.question_text: Which is an improper fraction?
fields.explanation: Improper fraction has numerator GREATER THAN OR EQUAL TO denominator. 8/5 → 8 > 5 ✓ improper fraction. All others (3/7, 1/4, 5/9) have numerator less than denominator — they are proper fractions.

fields.question_text: What is 3/4 - 1/3?
fields.explanation: LCM(4,3) = 12. 3/4 = 9/12 and 1/3 = 4/12. Subtract: 9/12 - 4/12 = 5/12. Always find LCM of denominators before adding or subtracting fractions with different denominators.

fields.question_text: What is 2/3 × 3/4?
fields.explanation: To multiply fractions: multiply numerators and multiply denominators. 2/3 × 3/4 = (2×3)/(3×4) = 6/12 = 1/2. Simplify: HCF(6,12)=6, so 6/12 = 1/2.

fields.question_text: Reciprocal of 3/7 is:
fields.explanation: Reciprocal means flipping the fraction — swapping numerator and denominator. Reciprocal of 3/7 = 7/3. When you multiply a fraction by its reciprocal, you always get 1. 3/7 × 7/3 = 21/21 = 1.

fields.question_text: If 2/5 of a number is 20, what is the number?
fields.explanation: 2/5 of number = 20. Number = 20 × 5/2 = 100/2 = 50. To find the whole, multiply by the reciprocal of the fraction. Check: 2/5 of 50 = (2×50)/5 = 100/5 = 20 ✓

fields.question_text: Arrange in ascending order: 1/2, 1/3, 1/4, 1/6
fields.explanation: For fractions with same numerator, larger denominator = smaller fraction. So 1/6 < 1/4 < 1/3 < 1/2. Ascending (smallest to largest): 1/6, 1/4, 1/3, 1/2.

fields.question_text: What is 3½ + 2¾?
fields.explanation: Convert: 3½ = 7/2 = 14/4 and 2¾ = 11/4. Add: 14/4 + 11/4 = 25/4 = 6¼. Or add whole parts: 3+2=5, add fractions: 1/2+3/4 = 2/4+3/4 = 5/4 = 1¼. Total = 5+1¼ = 6¼.

fields.question_text: A ribbon of 3/4 m is cut from 2m ribbon. What remains?
fields.explanation: Remaining = 2 - 3/4 = 8/4 - 3/4 = 5/4 m = 1¼ m. Convert 2 to quarters: 2 = 8/4. Then subtract 3/4. So 5/4 m = 1 and 1/4 metres of ribbon remains.

fields.question_text: Which fraction lies between 1/3 and 1/2?
fields.explanation: Convert to same denominator (LCM=30): 1/3=10/30, 1/2=15/30. We need fraction between 10/30 and 15/30. 2/5=12/30. Since 10<12<15, 2/5 lies between 1/3 and 1/2.

fields.question_text: What is 4/5 ÷ 2/3?
fields.explanation: To divide fractions, multiply by reciprocal. 4/5 ÷ 2/3 = 4/5 × 3/2 = 12/10 = 6/5. Rule: Keep first fraction, Change division to multiplication, Flip second fraction (KCF method).

fields.question_text: Shaded part of a pizza divided into 8 equal pieces where 3 pieces eaten is:
fields.explanation: Total pieces = 8. Eaten = 3. Remaining (shaded) = 8-3 = 5. Fraction remaining = 5/8. The question asks for shaded part which is the remaining pizza = 5/8.

fields.question_text: What is the place value of 3 in 4.375?
fields.explanation: In decimal 4.375, after decimal point: first place = tenths, second = hundredths, third = thousandths. 3 is in first decimal place (tenths). So place value of 3 = 3/10 = 0.3.

fields.question_text: Which decimal is equivalent to 1/4?
fields.explanation: 1/4 means 1 divided by 4. 1 ÷ 4 = 0.25. Or convert: 1/4 = 25/100 = 0.25. Common decimal equivalents to remember: 1/4=0.25, 1/2=0.5, 3/4=0.75, 1/5=0.2.

fields.question_text: What is 0.5 + 0.3?
fields.explanation: Add decimals by aligning decimal points. 0.5 + 0.3 = 0.8. Both have one decimal place. 5 tenths + 3 tenths = 8 tenths = 0.8. Just like 5+3=8, but in tenths place.

fields.question_text: Which is greatest: 0.3, 0.03, 0.33, 0.303?
fields.explanation: Compare digit by digit after decimal. All start with 0. Tenths: 0.3→3, 0.03→0, 0.33→3, 0.303→3. Three numbers tie at tenths. Hundredths: 0.33→3, 0.303→0, 0.3→0. So 0.33 is greatest.

fields.question_text: Convert 0.75 to fraction in simplest form.
What is 2.5 × 4?
fields.explanation: 2.5 × 4 = 10.0 = 10. Multiply as whole numbers: 25 × 4 = 100. Then place decimal: 2.5 has 1 decimal place, so answer has 1 decimal place = 10.0 = 10.

fields.question_text: 0.1 is equal to:
fields.explanation: 0.1 means 1 in the tenths place. Tenths place = 1/10. So 0.1 = 1/10. Similarly: 0.01 = 1/100, 0.001 = 1/1000. The number of decimal places tells the denominator power of 10.

fields.question_text: Which is smaller: 0.5 or 0.45?
fields.explanation: Compare: 0.50 and 0.45. Tenths place: 5 vs 4. Since 4 < 5, we have 0.45 < 0.50. So 0.45 is smaller than 0.5. Always add zeros to make equal decimal places before comparing.

fields.question_text: What is 5.6 - 2.3?
fields.explanation: Subtract by aligning decimal points. 5.6 - 2.3: subtract tenths 6-3=3, subtract ones 5-2=3. Answer = 3.3. Always align decimal points before adding or subtracting decimals.

fields.question_text: Express 7 cm in metres as decimal.
fields.explanation: 1 metre = 100 cm. So 7 cm = 7/100 m = 0.07 m. Remember: 1 cm = 0.01 m, 10 cm = 0.1 m, 100 cm = 1 m. Decimals are very useful in measurement conversions!

fields.question_text: What is 0.2 × 0.3?
fields.explanation: Multiply as whole numbers: 2 × 3 = 6. Count total decimal places: 0.2 has 1, 0.3 has 1. Total = 2 decimal places. So answer = 0.06 (put decimal 2 places from right in 6 → 0.06).

fields.question_text: What is 4.5 ÷ 0.5?
fields.explanation: 4.5 ÷ 0.5 = 45/10 ÷ 5/10 = 45/10 × 10/5 = 45/5 = 9. Or multiply both by 10: 45 ÷ 5 = 9. Dividing by 0.5 is same as multiplying by 2. 4.5 × 2 = 9.

fields.question_text: Ravi walks 1.35 km. Priya walks 2.7 km. Total distance?
fields.explanation: Total = 1.35 + 2.70 = 4.05 km. Align decimal points: 1.35 + 2.70. Add hundredths: 5+0=5. Add tenths: 3+7=10, write 0 carry 1. Add ones: 1+2+1=4. Answer = 4.05 km.

fields.question_text: Which decimal represents 3 ones, 4 tenths and 5 hundredths?
fields.explanation: 3 ones = 3, 4 tenths = 0.4, 5 hundredths = 0.05. Total = 3 + 0.4 + 0.05 = 3.45. Reading decimal: 3.45 = 3 ones, 4 tenths, 5 hundredths. Place value understanding is key!

fields.question_text: Cost of 1 kg rice is Rs. 36.50. Cost of 2.5 kg?
fields.explanation: Cost = 36.50 × 2.5. Multiply: 3650 × 25 = 91250. Count decimal places: 2+1=3. So 91250 → 91.250 = 91.25. Cost of 2.5 kg rice = Rs. 91.25.

fields.question_text: Convert 2 3/4 to decimal.
fields.explanation: 2 3/4 = 2 + 3/4. Convert 3/4 to decimal: 3÷4 = 0.75. So 2 3/4 = 2 + 0.75 = 2.75. Remember: 1/4=0.25, 2/4=0.50, 3/4=0.75. These are important conversions to memorize.

fields.question_text: Arrange in descending order: 1.1, 1.01, 1.11, 1.001
fields.explanation: Compare: all start with 1. Tenths: 1.1→1, 1.01→0, 1.11→1, 1.001→0. So 1.1 and 1.11 are bigger. Between them: hundredths 1.1→0, 1.11→1. So 1.11>1.1>1.01>1.001.

fields.question_text: What is 10.5 ÷ 100?
fields.explanation: Dividing by 100 shifts decimal point 2 places to the LEFT. 10.5 ÷ 100 = 0.105. Rule: ÷10 moves decimal 1 left, ÷100 moves 2 left, ÷1000 moves 3 left. Similarly ×10 moves right.

fields.question_text: Between which two whole numbers does 4.7 lie?
fields.explanation: 4.7 is between 4 and 5. The whole number part is 4, so it is greater than 4 but less than 5. On number line: 4 < 4.7 < 5. It is closer to 5 than to 4.

fields.question_text: A pencil is 12.5 cm. Express in mm.
fields.explanation: 1 cm = 10 mm. So 12.5 cm = 12.5 × 10 = 125 mm. Multiplying by 10 shifts decimal 1 place right. 12.5 × 10 = 125.0 = 125 mm. Always multiply by 10 to convert cm to mm.

fields.question_text: What is a tally mark?
fields.option_b: A way to count using marks
fields.explanation: Tally marks are a quick way to keep count. We make vertical marks (|) for each item and every 5th mark is drawn diagonally across the previous four (||||). This makes counting in groups of 5 easy.

fields.question_text: What does a bar graph use to show data?
fields.explanation: A bar graph uses rectangular bars to represent data. The length or height of each bar shows the value. Bars can be vertical or horizontal. Bar graphs are easy to read and compare data.

fields.question_text: What is the mode of: 2, 3, 3, 4, 5, 3, 2?
fields.explanation: Mode is the value that appears most often in a dataset. In 2,3,3,4,5,3,2 — count each: 2 appears 2 times, 3 appears 3 times, 4 appears 1 time, 5 appears 1 time. 3 appears most, so mode = 3.

fields.question_text: What is the range of: 5, 8, 12, 3, 9?
fields.explanation: Range = Largest value - Smallest value. Largest = 12, Smallest = 3. Range = 12 - 3 = 9. Range tells us how spread out the data is. Larger range means more spread out data.

fields.question_text: What is the arithmetic mean of 4, 8, 6, 10, 2?
fields.explanation: Mean = Sum of all values / Number of values. Sum = 4+8+6+10+2 = 30. Count = 5. Mean = 30/5 = 6. Mean is also called average. It represents the central value of data.

fields.question_text: In a pictograph, each picture represents 5 students. 3 pictures means?
fields.explanation: In pictograph, multiply number of pictures by the value each picture represents. 3 pictures × 5 students per picture = 15 students. Always check the key/legend of a pictograph first.

fields.question_text: What is the median of: 3, 7, 2, 9, 5?
fields.explanation: Median is the middle value when data is arranged in order. Arrange: 2,3,5,7,9. Middle value (3rd out of 5) = 5. Median = 5. For odd number of values, median is the middle number.

fields.question_text: The horizontal axis of a bar graph is called:
fields.explanation: A graph has two axes. The horizontal (left-right) axis is called X-axis. The vertical (up-down) axis is called Y-axis. They meet at a point called origin. X-axis usually shows categories, Y-axis shows values.

fields.question_text: Collection of information in numbers is called:
fields.explanation: Data is a collection of facts, numbers or information. Examples of data: marks scored by students, temperature recorded daily, number of cars passing on road. Data helps us understand patterns.

fields.question_text: Which graph uses pictures to represent data?
fields.explanation: Pictograph uses pictures or symbols to represent data. Each picture represents a fixed number of items (shown in key/legend). Pictographs are visually appealing and easy for children to understand.

fields.question_text: Mean of 5 numbers is 12. What is their sum?
fields.explanation: Mean = Sum / Count. So Sum = Mean × Count = 12 × 5 = 60. If average of 5 numbers is 12, their total sum must be 60. This reverse calculation is very useful in data problems.

fields.question_text: Scores: 85, 90, 78, 92, 88, 76, 95. What is the range?
fields.explanation: Range = Maximum - Minimum. Maximum score = 95, Minimum score = 76. Range = 95 - 76 = 19. The range of 19 tells us scores are spread over 19 marks.
Count each value: 4 appears 2 times, 5 appears 3 times, 6 appears 4 times, 7 appears 1 time. 6 appears most frequently (4 times). So mode = 6. A dataset can have more than one mode.
Arrange in order: 11,12,13,14,15,16,18. Count = 7 (odd). Middle position = (7+1)/2 = 4th value. 4th value = 14. Median = 14. Always arrange data in ascending order before finding median.

fields.question_text: A bar graph has scale 1 cm = 10 units. Bar of 4.5 cm represents?
fields.explanation: If scale is 1 cm = 10 units, then 4.5 cm = 4.5 × 10 = 45 units. Always multiply bar length by scale value to get actual data value. The scale is always mentioned on the Y-axis of bar graph.

fields.question_text: 6 students scored: 70,80,90,60,85,75. A new student scores 100. New mean?
fields.explanation: Old sum = 70+80+90+60+85+75 = 460. New sum = 460+100 = 560. New count = 7. New mean = 560/7 = 80. Adding a high score (100) raised the mean from 460/6=76.67 to 80.

fields.question_text: Which measure of central tendency is most affected by extreme values?
fields.explanation: Mean (average) is most affected by extreme values because it uses ALL values in calculation. Example: 1,2,3,4,100 — Mean=22 (pulled up by 100), Median=3 (unaffected). Mode is not affected at all.

fields.question_text: In a class of 40 students, 1/4 like cricket, 1/2 like football, rest like hockey. How many like hockey?
fields.explanation: Cricket = 1/4 of 40 = 10. Football = 1/2 of 40 = 20. Hockey = 40 - 10 - 20 = 10. Rest means remaining students. Always subtract known groups from total to find remaining group.

fields.question_text: Data: 3,6,3,6,7,8,3,6,7,9. Find mode and median.
fields.explanation: Count: 3 appears 3 times, 6 appears 3 times, 7 appears 2 times, 8 and 9 once each. Both 3 and 6 are modes (bimodal). Arrange: 3,3,3,6,6,6,7,7,8,9. Median = average of 5th and 6th = (6+6)/2 = 6.

fields.question_text: Pictograph: each symbol = 100 books. Library A has 3.5 symbols. How many books?
fields.explanation: 3.5 symbols × 100 books per symbol = 350 books. Half symbol (0.5) represents half the value = 50 books. So 3 full symbols = 300 books + 0.5 symbol = 50 books. Total = 350 books.

fields.question_text: What is the perimeter of a square with side 5 cm?
fields.explanation: Perimeter of square = 4 × side = 4 × 5 = 20 cm. Perimeter means total boundary length. A square has 4 equal sides, so just multiply one side by 4.

fields.question_text: What is the area of a rectangle 8 cm long and 5 cm wide?
fields.explanation: Area of rectangle = length × breadth = 8 × 5 = 40 sq cm. Area is measured in square units. Perimeter would be 2×(8+5) = 26 cm. Area tells us how much surface the shape covers.

fields.question_text: Perimeter of a rectangle with length 10 cm and breadth 6 cm?
fields.explanation: Perimeter of rectangle = 2 × (length + breadth) = 2 × (10 + 6) = 2 × 16 = 32 cm. A rectangle has 2 lengths and 2 breadths. So perimeter = 2l + 2b = 2(l+b).

fields.question_text: Area of a square with side 6 cm is:
fields.explanation: Area of square = side × side = side² = 6 × 6 = 36 sq cm. Square has all sides equal, so area = s². Perimeter would be 4×6 = 24 cm. Always write area in square units (sq cm or cm²).

fields.question_text: What is the unit of area?
fields.explanation: Area is measured in square units such as sq cm (cm²), sq m (m²), sq km (km²). Perimeter is measured in cm or m (linear units). Volume uses cubic units. Area always needs square units.

fields.question_text: A field is 25 m long and 15 m wide. Cost of fencing at Rs. 10 per metre?
fields.explanation: Perimeter = 2×(25+15) = 2×40 = 80 m. Cost = 80 × Rs. 10 = Rs. 800. Fencing covers the boundary (perimeter), not the area. Always use perimeter for fencing/boundary problems.

fields.question_text: Perimeter of equilateral triangle with side 9 cm?
fields.explanation: Equilateral triangle has all 3 sides equal. Perimeter = 3 × side = 3 × 9 = 27 cm. For any regular polygon, perimeter = number of sides × length of one side.

fields.question_text: 1 sq m = how many sq cm?
fields.explanation: 1 m = 100 cm. So 1 sq m = 1 m × 1 m = 100 cm × 100 cm = 10,000 sq cm. When converting area, square the conversion factor. 1 m² = 10,000 cm² = 10⁴ cm².

fields.question_text: Which has more area: square of 4 cm or rectangle of 6 cm × 2 cm?
fields.explanation: Square area = 4×4 = 16 sq cm. Rectangle area = 6×2 = 12 sq cm. Since 16 > 12, square has more area. Interesting: square has more area but rectangle has same perimeter! (Both = 16 cm perimeter).

fields.question_text: Perimeter of a regular hexagon with side 7 cm?
fields.explanation: Hexagon has 6 sides. Regular hexagon has all sides equal. Perimeter = 6 × side = 6 × 7 = 42 cm. For any regular polygon: perimeter = number of sides × length of each side.

fields.question_text: A rectangle has perimeter 40 cm and length 12 cm. Find breadth.
fields.explanation: Perimeter = 2(l+b). 40 = 2(12+b). 20 = 12+b. b = 20-12 = 8 cm. Breadth = 8 cm. Always use formula P = 2(l+b) and substitute known values to find unknown.

fields.question_text: Area of room is 120 sq m. Length is 15 m. Find breadth.
fields.explanation: Area = length × breadth. 120 = 15 × breadth. Breadth = 120/15 = 8 m. To find missing dimension: divide area by known dimension. Check: 15 × 8 = 120 sq m ✓

fields.question_text: Cost of tiling a floor 12m × 8m at Rs. 50 per sq m?
fields.explanation: Area of floor = 12 × 8 = 96 sq m. Cost = 96 × Rs. 50 = Rs. 4800. Tiling covers the area, not perimeter. Use area for carpeting, painting, tiling problems. Use perimeter for fencing, framing.

fields.question_text: A square garden has area 144 sq m. What is its perimeter?
fields.explanation: Area of square = side². 144 = side². Side = √144 = 12 m. Perimeter = 4 × side = 4 × 12 = 48 m. First find side from area, then calculate perimeter. √144 = 12 because 12×12=144.

fields.question_text: Perimeter of square equals perimeter of rectangle (8cm × 6cm). Find side of square.
fields.explanation: Perimeter of rectangle = 2×(8+6) = 2×14 = 28 cm. Perimeter of square = 28 cm. Side of square = 28/4 = 7 cm. Both shapes have same perimeter but different areas. Square area=49, Rectangle area=48.

fields.question_text: A path of 1 m width surrounds a garden 10m × 8m. Find area of path.
fields.explanation: Outer rectangle = (10+2) × (8+2) = 12 × 10 = 120 sq m. Inner garden = 10 × 8 = 80 sq m. Path area = Outer - Inner = 120 - 80 = 40 sq m. Add 2 to each dimension because path surrounds all sides.

fields.question_text: How many tiles of 20cm × 20cm needed to cover floor of 4m × 3m?
fields.explanation: Convert floor to cm: 4m = 400 cm, 3m = 300 cm. Floor area = 400 × 300 = 1,20,000 sq cm. Tile area = 20 × 20 = 400 sq cm. Number of tiles = 1,20,000 / 400 = 300 tiles.

fields.question_text: Perimeter of figure with sides 5,7,3,4,6 cm?
fields.explanation: Perimeter of any polygon = sum of all sides. 5+7+3+4+6 = 25 cm. For irregular polygons, simply add all side lengths. Perimeter formula 2(l+b) only works for rectangles.

fields.question_text: Rectangle has area 48 sq cm. If length is doubled and breadth halved, new area?
fields.explanation: Original area = l×b = 48. New length = 2l, New breadth = b/2. New area = 2l × b/2 = l×b = 48. Area remains same! Doubling length and halving breadth does not change area.

fields.question_text: Wire of 36 cm is bent into square. What is the area?
fields.explanation: Wire length = perimeter of square = 36 cm. Side = 36/4 = 9 cm. Area = side² = 9×9 = 81 sq cm. First find side from perimeter, then calculate area.

fields.question_text: In algebra, a letter used to represent unknown number is called:
fields.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing number. Example: in 2x+3, x is the variable. Constants are fixed numbers. Variables can take different values.

fields.question_text: If x = 3, what is 2x + 5?
fields.explanation: Substitute x = 3 in 2x+5. 2x+5 = 2×3+5 = 6+5 = 11. Always substitute the value and follow BODMAS — multiplication before addition.

fields.question_text: Which is an algebraic expression?
fields.explanation: An algebraic expression contains at least one variable (letter). 3x+7 has variable x — it is an algebraic expression. The others contain only numbers — they are arithmetic expressions, not algebraic.

fields.question_text: Solve: x + 7 = 12. What is x?
fields.explanation: x + 7 = 12. Subtract 7 from both sides. x = 12 - 7 = 5. Check: 5 + 7 = 12 ✓. Always do same operation on both sides to keep equation balanced.

fields.question_text: In 5n, the coefficient of n is:
fields.explanation: Coefficient is the number multiplied with the variable. In 5n, 5 is multiplied with n, so coefficient = 5. In just n (or 1n), coefficient = 1. Coefficient is always the numerical part.

fields.question_text: Age of Rahul is x years. Age 5 years later will be:
fields.explanation: If current age is x years, then after 5 years age = x + 5. We add 5 to present age to get future age. This is how algebra helps express real-life situations using variables.

fields.question_text: Solve: 3y = 18. What is y?
fields.explanation: 3y = 18. Divide both sides by 3. y = 18/3 = 6. Check: 3 × 6 = 18 ✓. To solve multiplication equations, divide both sides by the coefficient of the variable.

fields.question_text: Number of days in w weeks is:
fields.explanation: 1 week = 7 days. So w weeks = 7 × w = 7w days. This is a simple algebraic expression for real life. If w=2, then 7w = 14 days. If w=5, then 7w = 35 days.

fields.question_text: Which equation is correct for: A number increased by 8 gives 20?
fields.explanation: Increased by 8 means adding 8. A number (n) increased by 8 = n+8. This equals 20. So equation is n+8 = 20. Solving: n = 20-8 = 12. The number is 12.

fields.question_text: Value of expression 4p - 3q when p=2 and q=1?
fields.explanation: Substitute p=2 and q=1 in 4p-3q. 4p-3q = 4×2 - 3×1 = 8 - 3 = 5. When expression has two variables, substitute both values and calculate step by step.

fields.question_text: Solve: 2x - 3 = 7. What is x?
fields.explanation: 2x - 3 = 7. Add 3 to both sides: 2x = 10. Divide both sides by 2: x = 5. Check: 2×5-3 = 10-3 = 7 ✓. Two-step equations: first remove constant, then remove coefficient.

fields.question_text: Perimeter of equilateral triangle is 3a. If perimeter = 24, find a.
fields.explanation: 3a = 24. Divide both sides by 3. a = 24/3 = 8. So each side = 8 cm. Algebra helps us find unknown measurements in geometry problems easily.

fields.question_text: If price of 1 pen is Rs. p, cost of 5 pens and 3 notebooks (Rs. n each)?
fields.explanation: Cost of 5 pens = 5 × p = 5p. Cost of 3 notebooks = 3 × n = 3n. Total cost = 5p + 3n. This is an algebraic expression with two variables p and n representing different prices.

fields.question_text: Solve: x/4 = 6. What is x?
fields.explanation: x/4 = 6. Multiply both sides by 4. x = 6 × 4 = 24. Check: 24/4 = 6 ✓. To solve division equations, multiply both sides by the divisor.

fields.question_text: Sum of three consecutive numbers is 33. Find smallest number.
fields.explanation: Let smallest = n. Three consecutive: n, n+1, n+2. Sum = n+(n+1)+(n+2) = 3n+3 = 33. 3n = 30. n = 10. Numbers are 10,11,12. Check: 10+11+12 = 33 ✓.

fields.question_text: If 2x + 3 = x + 8, what is x?
fields.explanation: 2x+3 = x+8. Subtract x from both sides: x+3 = 8. Subtract 3: x = 5. Check: 2×5+3=13 and 5+8=13 ✓. When variables on both sides, move all variables to one side first.

fields.question_text: Father is 3 times son's age. Sum of ages is 48. Find son's age.
fields.explanation: Let son's age = x. Father's age = 3x. Sum: x + 3x = 48. 4x = 48. x = 12. Son is 12 years old, father is 36 years old. Check: 12+36=48 ✓ and 36=3×12 ✓.

fields.question_text: Which is NOT a linear equation?
fields.explanation: Linear equation has variable with power 1 only. x²+1=5 has x² (power 2) — this is NOT linear, it is quadratic. x+5=10, 2y=8, 3n-2=7 all have variables with power 1 — they are linear equations.

fields.question_text: Rectangle length is 3 more than breadth. Perimeter = 26 cm. Find breadth.
fields.explanation: Let breadth = b. Length = b+3. Perimeter = 2(l+b) = 2(b+3+b) = 2(2b+3) = 4b+6 = 26. 4b = 20. b = 5 cm. Length = 5+3 = 8 cm. Check: 2(8+5) = 2×13 = 26 ✓.

fields.question_text: A number when multiplied by 6 and then 4 subtracted gives 44. Find number.
fields.explanation: Let number = n. 6n - 4 = 44. Add 4: 6n = 48. Divide by 6: n = 8. Check: 6×8-4 = 48-4 = 44 ✓. Always form equation from word problem, then solve step by step.

fields.question_text: What is the ratio of 4 to 8?
fields.explanation: Ratio of 4 to 8 = 4:8. Simplify by dividing both by HCF(4,8)=4. 4/4 : 8/4 = 1:2. So ratio of 4 to 8 = 1:2. Always simplify ratio to lowest terms by dividing by HCF.

fields.question_text: Are 2:3 and 4:6 equivalent ratios?
fields.explanation: Yes! 2:3 = 2/3 and 4:6 = 4/6 = 2/3. Both equal 2/3 so they are equivalent ratios. To check: cross multiply 2×6=12 and 3×4=12. Equal products mean equivalent ratios.

fields.question_text: Ratio of boys to girls in class is 3:2. Total 30 students. How many girls?
fields.explanation: Total parts = 3+2 = 5. Girls = 2/5 of total = 2/5 × 30 = 12. Boys = 3/5 × 30 = 18. Check: 12+18 = 30 ✓ and ratio 18:12 = 3:2 ✓.

fields.question_text: If 5 pencils cost Rs. 15, what is cost of 8 pencils?
fields.explanation: Cost of 1 pencil = 15/5 = Rs. 3. Cost of 8 pencils = 8 × 3 = Rs. 24. This is unitary method — find value of 1 unit first, then multiply by required quantity.

fields.question_text: Which pair is in proportion: 2:3 and 8:12?
fields.explanation: Four numbers a:b::c:d are in proportion if a×d = b×c. Check: 2×12 = 24 and 3×8 = 24. Since 24=24, yes they are in proportion. Written as 2:3::8:12.

fields.question_text: What is ratio of 1 hour to 30 minutes?
fields.explanation: Convert to same unit first. 1 hour = 60 minutes. Ratio = 60:30 = 2:1. Always convert to same unit before finding ratio. Ratio has no units — it is just a comparison of two quantities.

fields.question_text: If a car travels 180 km in 3 hours, how far in 5 hours?
fields.explanation: Speed = 180/3 = 60 km per hour. Distance in 5 hours = 60 × 5 = 300 km. Using unitary method: 1 hour = 60 km, 5 hours = 300 km. Speed stays constant so this is direct proportion.

fields.question_text: Ratio of Rs. 5 to 50 paise is:
fields.explanation: Convert to same unit. Rs. 5 = 500 paise. Ratio = 500:50 = 10:1. Always use same unit before writing ratio. Rs. 5 is 10 times more than 50 paise, so ratio is 10:1.

fields.question_text: In proportion a:b::c:d, which is the product of means?
fields.explanation: In a:b::c:d — a and d are extremes, b and c are means. Product of means = b×c. Product of extremes = a×d. In proportion: product of means = product of extremes. So a×d = b×c.

fields.question_text: Divide Rs. 60 in ratio 2:3.
fields.explanation: Total parts = 2+3 = 5. First share = 2/5 × 60 = Rs. 24. Second share = 3/5 × 60 = Rs. 36. Check: 24+36=60 ✓ and 24:36=2:3 ✓. Always find total parts first.

fields.question_text: If 12 workers complete work in 8 days, how many days for 16 workers?
fields.explanation: More workers = less days (inverse proportion). 12 workers × 8 days = 16 workers × x days. 96 = 16x. x = 6 days. When one quantity increases and other decreases, it is inverse proportion.

fields.question_text: Find missing term: 3:5 = ?:20
fields.explanation: 3:5 = x:20. Cross multiply: 3×20 = 5×x. 60 = 5x. x = 12. Check: 3:5 = 12:20. Simplify 12:20 = 3:5 ✓. Use cross multiplication to find missing term in proportion.

fields.question_text: Map scale 1:50000. Distance on map is 3 cm. Actual distance?
fields.explanation: Scale 1:50000 means 1 cm on map = 50000 cm actual. 3 cm on map = 3×50000 = 150000 cm = 1500 m = 1.5 km. Always convert to required unit at end.

fields.question_text: Ages of A and B are in ratio 3:4. After 5 years ratio becomes 4:5. Find A's age.
fields.explanation: Let A=3x, B=4x. After 5 years: (3x+5):(4x+5) = 4:5. Cross multiply: 5(3x+5) = 4(4x+5). 15x+25 = 16x+20. x=5. A's age = 3×5 = 15 years. B's age = 4×5 = 20 years.

fields.question_text: Rs. 5600 divided among A, B, C in ratio 1:2:5. Find C's share.
A recipe needs 2 cups flour for 3 cups sugar. For 12 cups sugar, flour needed?
fields.explanation: Flour:Sugar = 2:3. For 12 cups sugar: 2/3 = x/12. x = (2×12)/3 = 24/3 = 8 cups flour. Using unitary method: 3 cups sugar needs 2 cups flour. 1 cup sugar needs 2/3 cup. 12 cups needs 12×2/3 = 8 cups.

fields.question_text: Speed of train is 60 km/h. Distance covered in 2.5 hours?
fields.explanation: Distance = Speed × Time = 60 × 2.5 = 150 km. Speed and distance are in direct proportion when time varies. More time = more distance at same speed. 60 km/h means 60 km in 1 hour.

fields.question_text: If 4:x = x:16, find x.
fields.explanation: 4:x = x:16 means x is the mean proportional. x² = 4×16 = 64. x = √64 = 8. Check: 4:8 = 8:16 = 1:2 ✓. When middle terms are equal in proportion, it is called mean proportion.

fields.question_text: Mixture has milk and water in ratio 5:2. Total mixture 35 litres. Find milk.
fields.explanation: Total parts = 5+2 = 7. Milk = 5/7 × 35 = 5×5 = 25 litres. Water = 2/7 × 35 = 10 litres. Check: 25+10=35 ✓ and 25:10=5:2 ✓.

fields.question_text: 15 men can build wall in 24 days. How many men needed to build in 9 days?
fields.explanation: Inverse proportion: more men = fewer days. 15 × 24 = x × 9. 360 = 9x. x = 40 men. Less time (9 days instead of 24) means we need more men. 40 men can complete same work in 9 days.

fields.question_text: A figure has line symmetry if:
fields.option_b: One half is mirror image of other half
fields.explanation: A figure has line symmetry when it can be folded along a line so that both halves match exactly. The folding line is called the line of symmetry or axis of symmetry. One half is the mirror image of the other.

fields.question_text: How many lines of symmetry does a circle have?
fields.explanation: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry because any diameter of a circle divides it into two equal mirror halves. Since infinite diameters can be drawn, a circle has infinite lines of symmetry.

fields.question_text: How many lines of symmetry does an equilateral triangle have?
fields.explanation: Equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry — one from each vertex to the midpoint of opposite side. Since all 3 sides are equal, all 3 lines divide it into equal mirror halves.

fields.question_text: Which letter has a vertical line of symmetry?
fields.explanation: Letter A has a vertical line of symmetry — fold it vertically and both halves match. Letters with vertical symmetry: A, H, I, M, O, T, U, V, W, X, Y. Letters F, G, P have no symmetry.

fields.question_text: How many lines of symmetry does a rectangle have?
fields.explanation: Rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry — one horizontal (through middle) and one vertical (through middle). Diagonals of rectangle are NOT lines of symmetry because folding along diagonal gives unequal halves.

fields.question_text: Which shape has NO line of symmetry?
fields.explanation: Scalene triangle has NO line of symmetry because all 3 sides are different lengths. No fold will make both halves match. Symmetric shapes: circle (infinite), square (4), rectangle (2), equilateral triangle (3).

fields.question_text: The line that divides a figure into two mirror halves is called:
fields.explanation: Line of symmetry (also called axis of symmetry) divides a figure into two identical mirror image halves. When folded along this line, both halves overlap perfectly. Every point has a mirror image on the other side.

fields.question_text: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
fields.explanation: Square has 4 lines of symmetry — 2 through midpoints of opposite sides (horizontal and vertical) and 2 through opposite corners (diagonals). All 4 divide square into equal mirror halves.

fields.question_text: Which of these has horizontal line of symmetry?
fields.explanation: Letter B has a horizontal line of symmetry — fold it horizontally and top half matches bottom half. Letters with horizontal symmetry: B, C, D, E, H, I, K, O, X. Letter A and M have vertical symmetry.

fields.question_text: An isosceles triangle has how many lines of symmetry?
fields.explanation: Isosceles triangle has exactly 1 line of symmetry — from the vertex between the two equal sides to the midpoint of the unequal side (base). Only this one fold creates two identical halves.

fields.question_text: How many lines of symmetry does a regular hexagon have?
fields.explanation: Regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry — 3 through opposite vertices and 3 through midpoints of opposite sides. Rule: regular polygon with n sides has n lines of symmetry.

fields.question_text: Which number has line symmetry when written?
fields.explanation: Number 8 has both horizontal and vertical lines of symmetry when written in standard form. It looks same when folded horizontally or vertically. Numbers 0 and 8 typically have symmetry.

fields.question_text: If a figure is reflected in a mirror, the image is:
fields.explanation: When a figure is reflected in a mirror, we get a laterally inverted image — left becomes right and right becomes left. The image is same size but flipped. This is called reflection symmetry.

fields.question_text: A regular polygon with n sides has how many lines of symmetry?
fields.explanation: A regular polygon with n sides has exactly n lines of symmetry. Triangle(3)→3 lines, Square(4)→4 lines, Pentagon(5)→5 lines, Hexagon(6)→6 lines. This is a general rule for all regular polygons.

fields.question_text: The word MATHEMATICS — how many letters have line symmetry?
fields.explanation: MATHEMATICS — check each: M(yes-vertical), A(yes-vertical), T(yes-vertical), H(yes-both), E(horizontal), I(both), C(horizontal), S(no). Letters with symmetry: M,A,T,H,E,I,C = many. Symmetric letters in word = M,A,T,H = 4 distinct symmetric letters appearing.

fields.question_text: Ink blot fold test — blot appears on both sides of fold. This shows:
fields.explanation: Ink blot test (Rorschach): fold paper with wet ink, unfold to get symmetric pattern. Both halves are mirror images of each other — this demonstrates reflection symmetry or line symmetry perfectly.

fields.question_text: Which has both horizontal AND vertical line of symmetry?
fields.explanation: Letter H has both vertical symmetry (left-right mirror) and horizontal symmetry (top-bottom mirror). Letters with both: H, I, O, X. F, J, L have no symmetry at all.

fields.question_text: A rhombus has how many lines of symmetry?
fields.explanation: Rhombus has 2 lines of symmetry — both diagonals are lines of symmetry. Note: unlike square, the lines joining midpoints of opposite sides are NOT lines of symmetry in rhombus. Only the 2 diagonals work.

fields.question_text: Which figure has rotational symmetry of order 4?
fields.explanation: Rotational symmetry order = number of times figure looks same in one full rotation. Square looks same at 90°,180°,270°,360° = 4 times = order 4. Equilateral triangle has order 3, rectangle has order 2.

fields.question_text: Mirror image of number 81 is:
fields.explanation: In mirror reflection, left and right are swapped. Mirror image of 81 = 18 (digits reversed and each digit flipped). Number 8 looks like 8 in mirror, number 1 looks like 1. But order reverses: 81 becomes 18.

fields.question_text: Which instrument is used to draw a circle?
fields.explanation: Compass is used to draw circles and arcs. One end has a pointed tip (kept at center) and other end has a pencil. Ruler measures and draws straight lines. Protractor measures angles.

fields.question_text: To draw a perpendicular bisector of a line segment, we use:
fields.explanation: Perpendicular bisector is drawn using compass and ruler together. Compass draws arcs from both endpoints, ruler connects the intersection points. This gives a line perpendicular to given segment at its midpoint.

fields.question_text: To construct an angle of 60°, we use:
fields.explanation: 60° angle can be constructed using compass and ruler without protractor. Draw a line, mark a point, draw arc, mark equal arc = equilateral triangle construction gives 60°. Compass and ruler are basic construction tools.

fields.question_text: What is a perpendicular bisector?
fields.option_b: Line that bisects and is perpendicular to segment
fields.explanation: Perpendicular bisector does TWO things: (1) bisects — divides segment into two equal halves, (2) perpendicular — meets the segment at 90°. Every point on perpendicular bisector is equidistant from both endpoints.

fields.question_text: Angle bisector divides an angle into:
fields.explanation: Angle bisector divides an angle into exactly two equal parts. If angle = 60°, bisector creates two angles of 30° each. Angle bisector can be drawn using compass and ruler accurately.

fields.question_text: To copy a line segment of length 5 cm, we need:
fields.explanation: To copy a line segment: use ruler to measure 5 cm, use compass to mark exact length and transfer it. Compass ensures exact copy without measurement error. Both tools together give accurate construction.

fields.question_text: A set square is used to draw angles of:
fields.explanation: Set squares come in two types: 30-60-90 set square (draws 30°, 60°, 90°) and 45-45-90 set square (draws 45°, 90°). Together they can draw 30°, 45°, 60°, 75°, 90° and their combinations.

fields.question_text: To construct 90° angle using compass, we first construct:
fields.explanation: To construct 90° using compass: draw a line, mark a point on it, draw perpendicular bisector at that point. The perpendicular bisector makes 90° with the line. This is the standard construction method.

fields.question_text: Arc means:
fields.explanation: Arc is a part of the circumference (boundary) of a circle. A minor arc is smaller part, major arc is larger part. When we draw with compass, we often draw arcs (partial circles) not full circles.

fields.question_text: To construct angle of 30°, we bisect which angle?
fields.explanation: 30° = 60° ÷ 2. Construct 60° first using compass (equilateral triangle method), then bisect it to get 30°. Similarly: 45° = 90°÷2, 15° = 30°÷2. Bisecting always halves the angle.

fields.question_text: Steps to construct perpendicular bisector — which is FIRST step?
fields.explanation: Steps for perpendicular bisector: (1) Draw line segment AB (2) Open compass more than half of AB (3) Draw arcs from A and B (4) Arcs intersect at two points P and Q (5) Join PQ — this is perpendicular bisector.

fields.question_text: To construct angle of 120°, we construct which angle first?
fields.explanation: 120° = 2 × 60°. Construct 60° first, then extend it to get 120°. Method: draw ray, mark two consecutive 60° angles, total = 120°. Or: 120° = 180° - 60°, construct 60° and take supplementary angle.

fields.question_text: When drawing perpendicular bisector, compass must be opened:
fields.explanation: Compass must be opened MORE than half the segment length. If opened exactly half or less, arcs from both ends will not intersect. Arcs must cross each other above and below the segment to get two intersection points.

fields.question_text: To construct 75°, we use combination of:
fields.explanation: 75° = 60° + 15° or 75° = 45° + 30°. Both combinations give 75°. Construct 60° and bisect remaining 30° to get 15°, add to 60°. Or construct 45° and 30° separately and add them.

fields.question_text: In construction, why do we use compass instead of measuring directly?
fields.option_c: Compass gives more accurate and exact construction
fields.explanation: Compass gives exact geometric constructions without measurement error. Ruler markings can have small errors. Geometric constructions with compass are mathematically precise and based on geometric properties, not measurement.

fields.question_text: How many arcs are drawn from each endpoint while constructing perpendicular bisector?
fields.explanation: One arc is drawn from each endpoint (total 2 arcs). With same compass opening, draw one arc from point A and one arc from point B. These 2 arcs intersect at 2 points. Join these intersection points to get perpendicular bisector.

fields.question_text: To bisect angle ABC, first step after drawing the angle is:
fields.option_a: Draw arc from B cutting both arms
fields.explanation: Steps to bisect angle ABC: (1) Draw arc from vertex B that cuts both arms BA and BC at points D and E (2) Draw equal arcs from D and E (3) They intersect at point F (4) Join BF — this is the angle bisector.

fields.question_text: Constructing angle of 135° — which combination is correct?
fields.explanation: 135° can be constructed as: 90°+45°, or 120°+15°, or 60°+75°. All give 135°. Most common method: construct 90° then bisect remaining 90° to get 45°, add = 135°. Or simply bisect 270° = 135°.

fields.question_text: Point equidistant from both endpoints of segment lies on:
fields.explanation: Any point equidistant from both endpoints of a segment lies on the perpendicular bisector of that segment. This is the key property of perpendicular bisector. The midpoint of segment also lies on perpendicular bisector.

fields.question_text: To construct triangle with sides 3cm, 4cm, 5cm — first step?
fields.explanation: To construct triangle: (1) Draw base = longest or given side (5cm) (2) From one end, draw arc of 3cm (3) From other end, draw arc of 4cm (4) Intersection of arcs = third vertex (5) Join to complete triangle.
